mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income growth and consumer spending, leading to negative corporate earnings revisions and equity declines.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or rolling IC < 0.03 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, which directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST yield positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in industrial production below -0.5% signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-negative in >70% of periods following an INDPRO drop below -0.5%, or signal loses predictive power during stable supply regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 3.0%, markets price in aggressive future monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations, generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are positive in >60% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth correlates with negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations, or rolling IC < 0.01 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions, simultaneous growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms robust demand momentum, leading to positive equity returns over the next quarter as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of expansion periods, or interaction effect vanishes in recessionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 110.0, it signals weakening manufacturing output that drags on broad index performance, triggering negative short-term equity returns due to sectoral weight exposure.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in >65% of periods when INDPRO drops below 110.0, or signal fails to predict direction in >70% of cases.
